programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Cameras capture footage of a ghostly figure in the window of Pablo Escobar's abandoned luxury building, mere moments before it is demolished, and some believe it was the notorious drug lord himself.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Cameras capture footage of a highway swarmed with snake-like creatures and an unexplained ooze; the latest tech reveals where they came from and their top-secret purpose.

programme.title: Strangest Things
programme.desc: The shattered skull of a 19th century railway worker is now one of the most astonishing objects in the history of medical science; experts explore this traumatic injury to reveal how it changed the field of neuroscience.

programme.title: Strangest Things
programme.desc: Containing intricately carved concentric circles, the Towie Ball is one of the finest Neolithic stoneworks ever discovered; using cutting-edge imaging technology, experts investigate the purpose behind this 5,000-year-old black stone mystery.

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.desc: Determining the age of the universe continues to be an elusive endeavor for scientists as their results are conflicting, and without a definitive answer, everything that's known about the cosmos could be completely wrong.

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.desc: As the two Voyager spacecraft reach interstellar space, experts explore their astonishing discoveries and how their extended operation has transformed into the ultimate mission.

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.desc: In a fictional scenario set in 2029, an asteroid hurtling towards Earth at 40,000 mph will reduce New York to ashes; examining the hypothetical, experts weigh in to determine what could happen, and how it might be stopped.

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.desc: New discoveries have revealed thousands of exoplanets beyond the solar system, and some resemble earth enough that one could be a new home for humanity; with cutting-edge technology, finding the perfect one is the scientific challenge of the age.

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.desc: Earth's history is marked by one cosmic disaster after another, forging the path of human evolution; now, experts explore how the Earth has teetered on the verge of destruction.

programme.title: If We Built It Today
programme.desc: For centuries, concert halls have been precisely designed and engineered to shape the best possible sound for acoustic performance; now, experts use state-of-the-art science to discover how the next generation of concert halls could be constructed.

programme.title: If We Built It Today
programme.desc: Machu Picchu is one of the greatest landmarks of world architecture, yet the purpose of its construction and how it was built still remains a mystery; experts use cutting-edge engineering to reveal what it would take to rebuild this legendary city.

programme.title: If We Built It Today
programme.desc: Stonehenge is an astonishing achievement of ancient construction that continues to baffle engineers thousands of years later; now, experts use the latest science to discover the logistics behind erecting another legendary monument today.

programme.title: If We Built It Today
programme.desc: The Parthenon is the crowning jewel of ancient Greek architecture and it was engineered according to a mysterious mathematical formula; expert builders examine the secrets of its design and investigate if modern-day methods could rebuilt it.

programme.title: Lost Cities of the Amazon
programme.desc: Experts investigate clues revealing evidence of a long-lost civilization in the Amazon that built giant cities of untold treasures.

programme.title: Lost Cities of the Amazon
programme.desc: Archaeologists are finding hard proof of the prehistoric civilization that once thrived in the Amazon; with the help of cutting-edge technology, these new discoveries reveal how these long-lost people built an advanced society in the jungle.

programme.title: Lost Cities of the Amazon
programme.desc: New discoveries reveal the mystery of what happened to the ancient superpower that once thrived in the Amazon jungles and, using cutting-edge archaeological methods, finding the reasons why the civilization fell.

programme.title: A Dispatch From Reuters
programme.desc: In 19th century England, journalist Paul Julius Reuter (Edward G. Robinson) and his partner, Max Wagner (Eddie Albert), utilize messenger pigeons to connect telegraph stations across Europe. As time passes and technology outdates his system, Reuter invents a wire system that transmits news stories directly to newspapers. programme.desc: A Belgian nun fond of making up her own songs, Sister Ann (Debbie Reynolds), pens a catchy tune for a sad young local boy (Ricky Cordell). Surprisingly, the song, "Dominique," becomes a hit after the resourceful Father Clementi (Ricardo Montalban) seeks the promotional assistance of music producer Robert Gerarde (Chad Everett). programme.title: The Nun's Story
programme.desc: Willful and outspoken Gabrielle van der Mal (Audrey Hepburn), the daughter of a prominent Belgian surgeon (Dean Jagger), leaves her upper-class existence to become a nun. As Sister Luke, she is eventually sent to the Belgian Congo, where she excels as a nurse working with a skilled doctor (Peter Finch). However, her willingness to continue as a nun is challenged when her father is killed by the Na

programme.title: A Dog of Flanders
programme.desc: Based on the famous 19th-century novel, this is the tale of Nello (David Ladd), a Flemish boy who dreams of becoming an artist. Nello is being raised by his grandfather, Jehan Daas (Donald Crisp), and the impoverished pair support themselves by delivering milk from a cart with the aid of their dog, Patrasche. programme.title: A Countess From Hong Kong
programme.desc: A Russian countess (Sophia Loren) stows away in the stateroom of a married U.S. diplomat (Marlon Brando) bound for New York.

programme.title: It Started in Naples
programme.desc: When American lawyer Michael Hamilton hears that his estranged younger brother and sister-in-law have died in a car accident, he flies to Naples, Italy, to settle their estate. Upon arriving, the xenophobic Hamilton meets his impish nephew, Nando, for the first time, and decides he will bring the boy back with him to the United States. programme.title: Marriage Italian Style
programme.desc: When handsome, successful Domenico (Marcello Mastroianni) first meets the sexy young waif Filomena (Sophia Loren) in Naples during World War II, he is instantly smitten. Flash forward to the postwar years, and the two meet again, sparking a passionate affair that spans two decades. But when Filomena -- who has now become Domenico's kept woman and has secretly borne his children -- learns that her

programme.title: Ghosts -- Italian Style
programme.desc: A crooked secret-admirer (Mario Adorf) lets a married couple (Sophia Loren, Vittorio Gassman) stay in his supposedly haunted palace.

programme.title: A Special Day
programme.desc: In Rome, fascist supporter Emanuele (John Vernon) attends a parade commemorating Adolf Hitler's historic meeting with Italian leader Benito Mussolini, leaving his apolitical wife, Antonietta (Sophia Loren), to tend to household duties. programme.title: MGM Parade
programme.desc: Robert Benchley; Tony Martin; host George Murphy.

programme.title: Made in Paris
programme.desc: A fashion buyer (Ann-Margret) sent to Paris attracts a charmer (Louis Jourdan), a newsman (Richard Crenna) and her boss' son.

programme.title: The Young Girls of Rochefort
programme.desc: Delphine (Catherine Deneuve) and Solange (Françoise Dorléac) are twin sisters who each want to find romance and leave their small seaside town of Rochefort, France. Soon they befriend a couple of visiting carnival workers who frequent their lonely mother's (Danielle Darrieux) café and hire the girls to sing in the carnival. Wanting a career as a songwriter, Solange falls for an American musician,

programme.title: The Swan
programme.desc: Prince Albert (Alec Guinness), who is scouring Europe for a wife, arrives in a tiny town to visit a banished family of nobles. Their matriarch, Beatrix (Jessie Royce Landis), wants her daughter, Alexandra (Grace Kelly), to marry Albert so that her family will be welcomed back into the kingdom. In order to rouse Albert's attention, Alexandra flirts with her attractive tutor, Dr. Agi (Louis Jourdan)

programme.title: Oklahoma!
programme.desc: There's a box social coming up, and Curly (Gordon MacRae) asks Laurey (Shirley Jones) if she'll be his date. Trouble is, Laurey thinks he's waited too long, and in a fit of pique accepts an offer from the boorish Jud (Rod Steiger) instead. Meanwhile, Laurey's friend Ado Annie (Gloria Grahame) is also torn between two men: a cowboy named Will (Gene Nelson) and a foreign peddler, Ali Hakim (Eddie Al

programme.title: The Secret Garden
programme.desc: Orphaned Mary Lennox (Margaret O'Brien) arrives at the Yorkshire mansion of her mysterious Uncle Craven (Herbert Marshall). Bitter and confused after the death of her parents, she clashes with the other residents of the estate, including the surly housekeeper (Gladys Cooper), the giggling maid (Elsa Lanchester) and Craven's invalid son (Dean Stockwell). After discovering a key to a secret garden,

programme.title: Picnic at Hanging Rock
programme.desc: In the early 1900s, Miranda (Anne Lambert) attends a girls boarding school in Australia. One Valentine's Day, the school's typically strict headmistress (Rachel Roberts) treats the girls to a picnic field trip to an unusual but scenic volcanic formation called Hanging Rock. Despite rules against it, Miranda and several other girls venture off. It's not until the end of the day that the faculty rea

programme.title: The Roman Spring of Mrs. Stone
programme.desc: Disheartened by suggestions that she is too old to for her role in a play, 50-year-old actress Karen Stone (Vivien Leigh) travels to Rome. After her husband dies on the plane, Karen rents an apartment and meets a conniving countess (Lotte Lenya) who introduces her to Paolo (Warren Beatty), a young Italian gigolo. Karen and Paolo begin a steamy affair, but the countess insists on paying for Paolo's

programme.title: One Way Passage
programme.desc: When Dan Hardesty (William Powell) meets Joan Ames (Kay Francis) in a Hong Kong bar, he doesn't let on that he's an escaped murderer, nor she that she's suffering from a fatal illness. After Dan is caught by Sergeant Steve Burke (Warren Hymer), the two head back to America via ocean liner, and spot Joan also aboard. Dan talks Steve into letting him out of the cuffs, and for the duration of the tri

programme.title: Confession
programme.desc: Lisa (Jane Bryan) finds herself being forcefully wooed by Michael Michailow (Basil Rathbone), a concert pianist many years her senior. In a nightclub, she finally succumbs to a kiss, and the sight causes the cabaret singer, Vera Kowalska (Kay Francis), to faint. As Michael attempts to beat a hasty retreat, Vera shoots him. When Vera has her day in court, she initially refuses to testify. But event

programme.title: Give Me Your Heart
programme.desc: In England, Belinda Warren (Kay Francis) falls in love and has an affair with married aristocrat Robert Melford (Patric Knowles), who refuses to leave his invalid wife, Rosamund (Frieda Inescort). Belinda gives birth to Robert's son, but unable to raise the child alone, lets Robert take him. Belinda flees to America where, in time, she marries a kind businessman, Jim Baker (George Brent). Years la

programme.title: Man Wanted
programme.desc: Hardworking magazine editor Lois (Kay Francis) is married to playboy Fred Ames (Kenneth Thomson). Her secretary abruptly quits just as salesman Tom Sheridan (David Manners) happens to come to her office to hawk his goods. Desperate for assistance, Lois impulsively hires Tom. While Fred continues his philandering ways, a romance blossoms between Tom and Lois. But Tom's fiancée grows suspicious of h

programme.title: My Bill
programme.desc: Mary (Kay Francis) was relatively well off, until her husband died in the middle of the Great Depression. Now she finds herself with four kids and quickly diminishing resources. She sends the three eldest children to stay with a wealthy aunt, but the youngest boy, Bill (Dickie Moore), remains with her. Mother and son try to make a go of it amidst the gossip and censure of the townsfolk. Fortunatel

programme.title: The Keyhole
programme.desc: Ann (Kay Francis), currently married to wealthy Schuyler Brooks (Henry Kolker), suddenly hears from her ex-husband, Maurice (Monroe Owsley), that he never completed the divorce proceedings and now wants hush money. Schuyler's sister (Helen Ware) advises Ann to sail to Cuba, thinking that Maurice will follow and then, since he is not American, be unable to re-enter the United States. However, Schuy

programme.title: The House on 56th Street
programme.desc: In turn-of-the-century New York, young Peggy (Kay Francis) falls in love with the wealthy Monte (Gene Raymond), and they move into a luxurious home. But, just after Peggy gives birth to a daughter, an old lover confronts her in a jealous rage. The argument turns violent, the lover dies, and Peggy is sent to jail. Monte is killed in World War I, and, when Peggy is released, she falls in with an amo

programme.title: The Amazing Dr. Clitterhouse
programme.desc: While conducting research on the physical impact of crime, Dr. Clitterhouse (Edward G. Robinson) decides to become a criminal himself. After a few jewelry thefts, the doctor meets Jo Keller (Claire Trevor), a major figure in the criminal underworld. programme.title: Valley of the Giants
programme.desc: A lumberman (Wayne Morris) and a gambling-house woman (Claire Trevor) thwart Easterners eyeing the California redwoods.

programme.title: Best of the Badmen
programme.desc: After the North defeats the South, Union Maj. Jeff Clanton heads to Missouri to provide the Confederacy's Quantrill's Raiders a chance to claim allegiance to the Union, thereby clearing their wanted status. But standing in Clanton's way are the corrupt lawmen Joad and Fowler, who would rather keep the men outlaws to collect the reward on their heads. After Joad and Fowler frame Clanton for murder,

programme.title: Borderline
programme.desc: The Los Angeles police know that Pete Ritchie (Raymond Burr) has been bringing drugs into the city, but they can't pin a single piece of evidence on him. After many botched attempts to get at the crook, they resort to having cop Madeleine (Claire Trevor) go undercover and seduce her way into Ritchie's circle. Before she can get anywhere, she's abducted by Johnny (Fred MacMurray), a government agen

programme.title: Born to Kill
programme.desc: Drifter Sam Wilde (Lawrence Tierney) murders a Reno, Nev., boardinghouse owner and her boyfriend in a fit of jealousy. Tenant Helen Brent (Claire Trevor) discovers the bodies, but instead of reporting the crime, she leaves for San Francisco as planned to return to her wealthy fiancé. On the same train, Sam flirts with Helen, then tracks her to her half-sister's house, where he continues to pursue

programme.title: Murder, My Sweet
programme.desc: Gumshoe Philip Marlowe (Dick Powell) is hired by the oafish Moose Malloy (Mike Mazurki) to track down his former girlfriend. He's also hired to accompany an effeminate playboy buy back some jewels. When the exchange results in the playboy's murder, Marlowe can't leave the case alone, and soon discovers it's related to Malloy's. As he gets drawn deeper into a complex web of intrigue by a mysterious

programme.title: Key Largo
programme.desc: This classic film noir by John Huston stars Humphrey Bogart as World War II vet Frank McCloud. Visiting Key Largo to pay his respects to the family of his late war buddy, McCloud attempts to comfort his comrade's widow, Nora, and father, James Temple, who operate a hotel. But McCloud realizes that mobsters, led by the infamous Johnny Rocco, are staying in the hotel. When the criminals take over th

programme.title: Stagecoach
programme.desc: John Ford's landmark Western revolves around an assorted group of colorful passengers aboard the Overland stagecoach bound for Lordsburg, New Mexico, in the 1880s. programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: Nurse Nellie Forbush (Mitzi Gaynor) of the U.S. Navy falls for middle-aged French plantation owner Emile De Becque (Rossano Brazzi), but recoils upon discovering that he's fathered two mixed-race children. programme.title: Fanny
programme.desc: Young lovers Fanny (Leslie Caron) and Marius (Horst Buchholz) seem made for each other, but he also wants to set out to sea. Eventually, Marius departs and, after years, they are finally reunited, only for him to discover that he is the father of Fanny's son. programme.title: La reina del flow
programme.desc: Yeimy Montoya paga una injusta condena en Nueva York tras haber sido engañada por el hombre que amaba. Por lo tanto, hace un pacto con las autoridades judiciales para capturar al hombre que asesinó a su familia.

programme.title: Pantanal
programme.desc: José Leôncio, hacendado del Pantanal, deja a una joven embarazada en una visita a Río de Janeiro. Ella va a vivir con él al Pantanal, pero es infeliz y decide volver a la ciudad con su hijo. Veinte años después, este va en busca de su padre.

programme.title: Jesús
programme.desc: La trayectoria de Jesucristo antes de la resurrección. Sus milagros y el calvario de la cruz. Las vidas de quienes fueron curados por Jesucristo y quiénes eran los apóstoles.

programme.title: Destinos cruzados
programme.desc: Toprak despierta de un coma tras cinco años sin recordar nada de su pasado, y regresa a una familia que no reconoce, incluida su prometida de toda la vida y un niño que vive con él. Sin embargo, su amnesia lo mantiene en una incertidumbre constante.

programme.title: La luz de mi vida
programme.desc: Tras la muerte de su esposo Hakan, Elif descubre que él llevaba una doble vida y tenía una hija con una mujer llamada Firat.

programme.title: Bajo el mismo cielo
programme.desc: La historia de Carlos Martínez, un padre mexicano indocumentado que vive en Los Ángeles, un hombre trabajador y honrado que lucha para darle una mejor vida a sus dos hijos Luis y Rodrigo, pero su mayor temor es ser deportado y perder a su familia.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Grace begins to have cold feet as her relatives start arriving for her wedding, while Will tries to overcome his mixed emotions.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will is uneasy about the whole idea of Grace marrying Leo (guest star Harry Connick Jr.). Debbie Reynolds and Katie Couric also guest star.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will invites Karen to move in with him after she is kicked out of the Plaza Hotel; Elton John is an all-seeing arbiter of gay style.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Grace has only two tickets to "The Nutcracker" and cannot decide whether to take Will or Leo. Mary McDonough guest stars.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Jack convinces Will to join a gay soccer team; Karen is giddy in anticipation of her date with a handsome restaurateur (Andy Garcia).

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Grace is puzzled by her new neighbor -- a bizarre bohemian woman.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will and Jack coach Karen's cousin (Dan Futterman) on his new gay lifestyle, reminding Will of his own experience of coming out in 1986.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Karen's rival (Minnie Driver) attempts to manipulate Jack; Will and Grace revitalize their friendship with a night of board games.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Jack encounters his former babysitter (guest star Demi Moore) and lapses into childlike behavior. Rosanna Arquette and Leigh-Allyn Baker also guest star.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will and Jack's transformation of Karen's cousin Barry is complete; Karen duels with Beverly during a silent auction for charity.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Jealous Will spots Barry (Dan Futterman) disappearing with Jack; Karen trades places with Rosario to woo a handsome handyman (Bruno Campos).

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will forms an anti-romance club with Karen and his boss (Gene Wilder).

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will attempts to surprise Grace for her birthday by sneaking Leo back from Africa; Jack and Karen take their mission to keep Leo's return secret too seriously.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Karen's search for a roommate leads to a high-maintenance individual, who shares many of her eccentricities; Grace and Jack intervene when Will uses painkillers as a crutch.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Karen hires a somewhat immature divorce attorney (guest star Macaulay Culkin) to oppose Will in court.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: When Karen's ex-husband dies, she and her British rival (Minnie Driver) have a contest of wills; Leo (Harry Connick Jr.) is needed in Guatemala.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Karen confronts her ex-husband's mistress before inviting everybody for a ceremony on her yacht; Grace suffers from jealousy after meeting her husband's sexy co-worker.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will and Jack awake in a precarious position while at sea; Grace discovers love letters in Leo's bag from a female colleague.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Grace is vexed when she learns that Leo's ex-girlfriend (Mira Sorvino) is intimately acquainted with Will.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Karen invites Grace and Leo to play tennis at her exclusive club, but her fierce playing style drives Grace away.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Jack lands a speaking role opposite James Earl Jones in an off-Broadway play and gives the veteran actor some pointers.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will takes Leo on shopping sprees and for high tea to help him rekindle his love of the city; Karen coaches Jack for the annual gay spelling bee.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will takes his mother for an afternoon stroll and bumps into an attractive bachelor (Dylan McDermott); Karen continues her lifelong quest to hunt down hubby-stealing Lorraine.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: An unorthodox doctor (Jack Black) gives Karen her annual physical; Jack begs Will to support his decision to become a student nurse.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will urges Grace to stay in New York so they can spend some time together; Karen strong-arms Jack's nursing teacher into letting him skip class but still make the grade.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will catches the eye of a female diner after being stood up; Grace bumps into a man (Tom Everett Scott) with whom she once shared a forgettable encounter; Karen runs into her nemesis, Candice Bergen.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: A weak bladder forces Will to entrust Grace with his place in line for Barry Manilow tickets; Grace's mother (Debbie Reynolds) cancels their annual mother-daughter shopping trip to have dinner with Jack.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Grace's freeloading sister (Geena Davis) comes to town to launch her latest business enterprise; Karen spurns Lyle (John Cleese), then changes her mind after Lorraine (Minnie Driver) expresses her disapproval.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: A rich plastic surgeon (guest star Hal Linden) tries to buy Will's affection; Grace boycotts a new restaurant.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will's new client (Dave Foley) falls for Jack; Grace and Karen discover hidden money while trying to fix the plumbing in Leo's cabin.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will is the only single man in a cooking class full of couples, including Jack and his new boyfriend (Dave Foley); Karen's mother (Suzanne Pleshette) asks Grace to decorate her apartment.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will and Grace begin a business venture selling apartments; Jack runs into his former acting mentor, Zandra; Karen and Lyle (John Cleese) try to renew romance.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will and Grace try to break up Jack and Stuart (Dave Foley) so they can sell the apartment for a higher price; Karen demands that Lyle (John Cleese) discipline his daughter (Minnie Driver).

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Real-estate entrepreneurs Will and Grace face formidable competition on the East Side from a lesbian couple (Edie Falco, Chloe Sevigny); John Edward gives Karen some psychic guidance.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Jack and Grace stumble upon Stuart (Dave Foley) watching a movie matinee in the company of another man; Will tries to teach Karen how to drive.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Grace advises Will to play hard to get with his new boyfriend (Bobby Cannavale); Jack and Karen despair after learning their favorite TV series will be going off the air.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will introduces his new boyfriend (Bobby Cannavale) to overly critical Grace; Karen tells Jack that their friendship will change after her wedding.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: At Tina's (Lesley Ann Warren) urging, Will tries to find out if there is a new woman in his father's life; Jack's son decides to try out for the high-school cheerleading squad.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: On the eve of his graduation from nursing school, Jack turns to Will for help writing his commencement address.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Karen moves her wedding to Las Vegas, where Jennifer Lopez agrees to sing at the ceremony.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Karen moves her wedding to Las Vegas, where Jennifer Lopez agrees to sing at the ceremony.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Karen recruits Jack to help her get Jennifer Lopez to listen to Karen's new song; Grace takes out her frustration over her cheating husband on Will.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Jack wangles his way into Janet Jackson's (guest starring as herself) performance tour as a backup dancer.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: As she attempts to steal doughnuts at an AA meeting, Grace has a surprise reunion with Val (Molly Shannon); Will and Jack attend a gay television focus group meeting.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Grace accuses Will of being too nice to strangers; Jack has trouble at his new company when his co-workers mock him about his Cher doll.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Despite the fact that Will has never had a happy birthday, his new relationship with Vince (Bobby Cannavale) gives him hope that this one will be.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will convinces Jack to aid a local gay-bookstore owner to save his store from closing.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will and Grace decide to invite Vince and Nadine (Kristin Davis) over, but they quickly learn that, despite similarities in friendship, they are polar opposites.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: After a great date with someone else, Grace comes home to a garbled message from Leo; Karen and Jack try to set each other up, but things go awry.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will, Grace, Jack and Karen go on a road trip for Grace's getaway weekend, but Jack and Karen hate the car ride and bail on the plan.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Vince's mother cannot make her traditional holiday feast, so Will volunteers to make a special meal, but Vince's mother refuses to give him any credit; Jack gives Vince's sister (Jamie-Lynn DiScala) pointers on keeping secrets.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Vince's mother is unable to make her traditional holiday feast, so Will volunteers to make a special meal, but Vince's mother refuses to give Will any of the credit; Jack gives Vince's sister (Jamie-Lynn DiScala) pointers on keeping secrets.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Grace accidentally breaks a treasured figurine at Will's mother's (Blythe Danner) holiday party and blames it on another guest.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: An old classmate (Jeff Goldblum) tries to get revenge on Karen by becoming the president of Walker Inc.; Will and Grace run into Vince and a co-worker while playing hooky from work.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will is reluctant to bring Vince to a dinner party at his boss's (Lily Tomlin) home, fearing it will scratch his chances of making partner at the firm.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: When Scott Woolley (Jeff Goldblum) tries to get Grace to fire Karen by impersonating a restaurant owner, the women plan a trap for him.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Although Grace tries to ignore St. Valentine's Day, she winds up with a hunky greeting-card writer (Ed Burns); Karen's former nemesis (Jeff Goldblum) tries to win her heart by showering her with gifts.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Grace drags Will along on her first date with Nick (Edward Burns); Karen tries to set Jack up on a date with the neighborhood bird enthusiast (Luke Perry), but things don't quite go as she has planned.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: When Will agrees to fire Karen's pastry chef, he becomes involved in a love triangle that the baker created.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: When Jack convinces Will that they need a change of scenery, they buy an apartment in an up-and-coming gay community.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will is jealous when his no-nonsense therapist (guest star Sharon Stone) becomes fast friends with Grace.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Grace convinces Karen to attend her father's (Alan Arkin) birthday party; Karen entertains herself by spending time with Martin's friend Burt (Lee Majors).

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Grace's near-death experience prompts her to write her will and jolts Will into making a life-altering career choice.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: When Will's writing career fizzles, Malcolm (Alec Baldwin) makes him another job offer; Grace's old college flame (Eric Stoltz) gives her a job decorating his new hotel.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will becomes suspicious of his new job when he finds himself in an empty office with a German shepherd and a Russian-speaking thug.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Grace contemplates an affair with a married man (Eric Stoltz); Jack begins his new career as a talk-show host; Will continues to hide from Karen the fact that Stan is alive.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: When Will suspects that Karen is more upset about Stan being alive than she is letting on, he makes it his mission to help her heal emotionally.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Jack learns that Will has never learned how to swim, so he makes it his mission to teach him.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Jack tells Karen he believes she belongs with Stan, not Malcolm (Alec Baldwin).

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Jack brings Baby Glenn (Jason Biggs), who gained notoriety in the '80s after being trapped in a hole for days, to Will's charity auction and carnival.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Grace and Will spot her ex-husband, Leo, on their plane to London; Will charms a pair of gay flight attendants into letting Grace into first-class to talk with Leo.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Jack's decision to move makes Will take a close look at their friendship; Karen and Grace are invited to the memorial celebration for Beverly's (Leslie Jordan) deceased wife.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: When Grace finds it impossible to please her mother, Jack offers to help, believing no one knows how to be a good daughter like a gay son.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: When the gang spends Christmas at the home of Will's mother, Marilyn Truman (Blythe Danner), Grace gets romantic with Will's brother.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: "The Sound of Music Sing-Along" has come to town and no one is more excited than Grace; she plans for her and Will to attend the musical in character-like style by dressing up in costumes.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: All of New York society attend Karen's birthday party, but Will, Grace and Jack embarrass themselves and get stuck in the bathroom with the birthday girl.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will insists that Grace turn down a job offer from his former boss, Margot, who has asked Grace to redecorate the law firm.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will and Grace dread a dinner date with Rob and Ellen, but the identity of their waiter (Bobby Cannavale) eclipses their friends' big news.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will, Karen and Grace travel to Los Angeles to support Jack as he accepts an award for Best Local Cable Guy Talk Show Host.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Karen ignores Grace's plea for a low-key wedding and plans a huge affair, hiring Hall & Oates to perform "Maneater" at the ceremony.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Nervous but optimistic Grace decides it is time to tell Leo (Harry Connick Jr.) she is pregnant with his child.

programme.title: Will & Grace

programme.title: Live: MLB Central
programme.desc: MLB Network's first-ever regular-season morning show features hosts Robert Flores, Mark DeRosa and Lauren Shehadi reporting from Studio 21, named in honor of Hall of Famer and humanitarian Roberto Clemente. Updates and context on the latest news, extensive on-field highlights, and long-form conversations with guests, celebrities and insiders from around baseball are presented each weekday morning.

programme.title: Live: MLB Now
programme.desc: The hourlong weekday afternoon "MLB Now" presents the day's top stories in baseball by spanning the sport's cultural divide. Host Brian Kenny discusses the issues and provides analysis, with a bent toward modern analytics to prove his points. The series also features interviews with personalities throughout the game, and viewer opinions are welcome as well via social media polls.

programme.title: Charade
programme.desc: A suave stranger (Cary Grant) helps a chic widow (Audrey Hepburn) stalked by four men looking for loot in Paris.

programme.title: All About Eve
programme.desc: A Broadway star (Bette Davis) takes a young and seemingly naive aspiring actress (Anne Baxter) under her wing.

programme.title: Let's Make Love
programme.desc: A billionaire (Yves Montand) plays himself in an off-Broadway satire and falls for his co-star (Marilyn Monroe).

programme.title: Come to the Stable
programme.desc: Two French nuns (Loretta Young, Celeste Holm) in New England raise funds for a children's hospital in a town called Bethlehem.

programme.title: The Bible
programme.desc: Book of Genesis stories include Adam (Michael Parks) and Eve, Abraham (George C. Scott), and the Tower of Babel.

programme.title: Rebecca of Sunnybrook Farm
programme.desc: After Rebecca is mistakenly rejected at an audition for a radio advertising campaign, her stepfather takes her upstate.

programme.title: The Second Time Around
programme.desc: A 1912 New York widow (Debbie Reynolds) with children becomes the umbrella-toting sheriff of a wild Arizona town.

programme.title: A Man Called Sledge
programme.desc: Two outlaws (James Garner, Dennis Weaver) stage a prison break to steal a fortune in gold dust guarded by the Army.

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in the West
programme.desc: An outlaw (Henry Fonda) working for a railroad magnate fights a stranger (Charles Bronson) for a New Orleans widow's (Claudia Cardinale) land.

programme.title: The Sand Pebbles
programme.desc: Multiple Oscar nominations went to this tale of a cynical sailor's experiences on an American gunboat in 1926 China.

programme.title: A Midnight Clear
programme.desc: A young GI (Ethan Hawke) and his squad have a strange encounter with Germans in the Ardennes forest, December 1944.

programme.title: The Robe
programme.desc: The red robe worn by Christ links a Roman tribune (Richard Burton), his Christian lover (Jean Simmons) and a Greek slave (Victor Mature).

programme.title: Demetrius and the Gladiators
programme.desc: A Greek slave (Victor Mature) entrusted with Christ's robe must play gladiator for Emperor Caligula and Messalina.

programme.title: I Deal in Danger
programme.desc: Rogue spy David March (Robert Goulet) defects to the Germans in the early days of World War II, but, unbeknownst to his Nazi handlers, he's really a double agent feeding information to the United States, which has not yet officially entered the war.

programme.title: The Eagle Has Landed
programme.desc: A Nazi colonel (Robert Duvall) drops a colonel (Michael Caine), a task force and an Irishman (Donald Sutherland) into England to kidnap Winston Churchill.

programme.title: Fractured Flickers
programme.desc: Hans Conried and a celebrity guest present a succession of film clips from the silent picture era dubbed over with comedic dialogue and sound effects.

programme.title: The Little Princess
programme.desc: A poor but proud girl searches army hospitals for her father, reported dead in the Boer War. With her father missing in action, the Victorian child is sent to a harsh boarding school. She escapes the school to find her father.

programme.title: Captain January
programme.desc: An orphan (Shirley Temple) lives with a lighthouse keeper (Guy Kibbee) until a truant officer takes her away.

programme.title: Curly Top
programme.desc: A Park Avenue millionaire (John Boles) adopts a frisky orphan (Shirley Temple) and her teenage sister (Rochelle Hudson).

programme.title: Wee Willie Winkie
programme.desc: An American girl (Shirley Temple) charms hostile natives and a Highland soldier (Victor McLaglen) in colonial India.

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: An aunt takes a Swiss orphan (Shirley Temple) from the girl's grandfather (Jean Hersholt) in the Swiss Alps and puts her to work in Frankfurt as companion to a disabled child.

programme.title: Stowaway
programme.desc: An orphan (Shirley Temple) attaches herself to an American playboy (Robert Young) and his sweetheart (Alice Faye) in Shanghai.

programme.title: Rebecca of Sunnybrook Farm
programme.desc: After Rebecca is mistakenly rejected at an audition for a radio advertising campaign, her stepfather takes her upstate.

programme.title: The Best of Everything
programme.desc: A Manhattan career woman (Joan Crawford) and her publishing-house colleagues have affairs with married men.

programme.title: Compulsion
programme.desc: In a retelling of the Leopold and Loeb murders, two men murder a younger boy and are subsequently put on trial.

programme.title: Whatever Happened to Aunt Alice?
programme.desc: An Arizona widow (Geraldine Page) bilks her maids, kills them and uses them for garden fertilizer; Alice (Ruth Gordon) finds out.

programme.title: The Exorcist III
programme.desc: A police detective (George C. Scott) investigates a series of slayings done in the style of an executed killer (Brad Dourif).
